name: Jalen Hurts
headline: Buy the dip on a rushing-fueled QB1
body:
Hurts finished QB16 in 2025 with 301.1 fantasy points, his quietest line in years, and the advanced profile explains the cooling. His EPA per play slid to 0.074 from 0.153 the prior season, success rate dipped to 44.3%, and CPOE fell to 3.2 from 7.5. Most telling for fantasy: his ground game shrank to 421 rushing yards and eight scores on 105 carries, down from 630 and 14 the year before. He still threw 25 touchdowns against just six picks and hit 40 passes of 20-plus yards, so the arm held up even as the legs went quiet.

The rushing dropoff tracks with offensive-line injuries that derailed the back half of the season; healthy bookends should restore the designed-run volume that anchors his ceiling. Losing A.J. Brown stings, but DeVonta Smith, Dallas Goedert and a reshuffled corps keep enough around him, and the schedule grades out as the league's softest. At a QB6-7 price near pick 72, the rebound math is favorable.
